methods.name: available
methods.docs: Checks whether the native speech recognition service is usable on the current device.

methods.name: isOnDeviceRecognitionAvailable
methods.docs:
Checks whether the platform's newer on-device recognition path is available for the selected locale.

This is the capability check you should use before enabling `useOnDeviceRecognition`.
A `true` result means the current device, OS version, and locale can use the newer
on-device path for that platform.

Returns `false` when the device only supports the legacy recognizer path.

Platform SDK docs:
iOS: [Speech](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/speech)
Android: [SpeechRecognizer](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/speech/SpeechRecognizer)

methods.name: start
methods.docs:
Begins capturing audio and transcribing speech.

When `partialResults` is `true`, the returned promise resolves immediately and updates are
streamed through the `partialResults` listener until the session ends.

The default path keeps the legacy recognizer behavior for backward compatibility.
Pass `useOnDeviceRecognition: true` only after checking
{@link SpeechRecognitionPlugin.isOnDeviceRecognitionAvailable}.

methods.name: stop
methods.docs: Stops listening and tears down native resources.

methods.name: forceStop
methods.docs:
Force stops the current session.

On Android, this first tries a normal stop and then falls back to destroy/recreate after `timeout`.
On iOS, the current session is stopped immediately.

If a partial transcript is cached, it is emitted through the `partialResults` listener with `forced: true`.

methods.name: getLastPartialResult
methods.docs: Gets the last cached partial transcription result.

methods.name: setPTTState
methods.docs:
Updates the current push-to-talk button state.

Use this together with `continuousPTT` or with a custom hold-to-talk flow.

methods.name: getSupportedLanguages
methods.docs:
Gets the locales supported by the underlying recognizer.

Android 13+ devices no longer expose this list; in that case `languages` is empty.

methods.name: isListening
methods.docs: Returns whether the plugin is actively listening for speech.

methods.name: requestPermissions
methods.docs: Requests the microphone + speech recognition permissions.

methods.name: getPluginVersion
methods.docs:
Returns the native plugin version bundled with this package.

Useful when reporting issues to confirm that native and JS versions match.

methods.name: addListener
methods.docs: Listen for segmented session completion events (Android only).

methods.name: addListener
methods.docs: Listen for segmented recognition results (Android only).

methods.name: addListener
methods.docs: Listen for partial transcription updates emitted while `partialResults` is enabled.

methods.name: addListener
methods.docs: Listen for changes to the native listening state.

methods.name: addListener
methods.docs: Listen for the recognizer becoming ready for another session.

interfaces.name: SpeechRecognitionStartOptions
interfaces.docs: Configure how the recognizer behaves when calling {@link SpeechRecognitionPlugin.start}.

properties.name: language
properties.docs: Locale identifier such as `en-US`. When omitted the device language is used.

properties.name: maxResults
properties.docs: Maximum number of final matches returned by native APIs. Defaults to `5`.

properties.name: prompt
properties.docs: Prompt message shown inside the Android system dialog (ignored on iOS).

properties.name: popup
properties.docs:
When `true`, Android shows the OS speech dialog instead of running inline recognition.
Defaults to `false`.

properties.name: partialResults
properties.docs: Emits partial transcription updates through the `partialResults` listener while audio is captured.

properties.name: addPunctuation
properties.docs: Enables native punctuation handling where supported (iOS 16+).

properties.name: contextualStrings
properties.docs:
Words or phrases that should be recognized more accurately by native speech APIs.

On iOS, these are passed to `SFSpeechRecognitionRequest.contextualStrings`
when the plugin uses the legacy `SFSpeechRecognizer` path. That path is the
default on all iOS versions, the fallback below iOS 26, and still available
on iOS 26+ by leaving `useOnDeviceRecognition` disabled.

Ignored by Android and by the iOS 26+ `SpeechAnalyzer` path.

properties.name: useOnDeviceRecognition
properties.docs:
Opt in to the platform's newer on-device recognition path when available.

On iOS 26+, this uses Apple's `SpeechAnalyzer` / `SpeechTranscriber` pipeline.
On recent Android versions, this uses the on-device `SpeechRecognizer` path.

It is intentionally opt-in so existing apps keep the legacy flow unless they choose
to roll out the new behavior.
On iOS, leaving this disabled keeps `SFSpeechRecognizer` on every supported OS version.
Enabling it on older iOS versions or unsupported locales falls back to `SFSpeechRecognizer`.

Use {@link SpeechRecognitionPlugin.isOnDeviceRecognitionAvailable} before enabling it in production.

Platform SDK docs:
iOS: [Speech](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/speech),
[SpeechAnalyzer](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/speech/speechanalyzer),
[SpeechTranscriber](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/speech/speechtranscriber)
Android: [SpeechRecognizer](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/speech/SpeechRecognizer)

Defaults to `false`.

properties.name: allowForSilence
properties.docs:
Allow a number of milliseconds of silence before splitting the recognition session into segments.
Required to be greater than zero and currently supported on Android only.

properties.name: continuousPTT
properties.docs:
EXPERIMENTAL: Keep a PTT session alive across silence by restarting recognition while the button stays held.

This restart behavior is implemented for Android inline recognition and iOS native recognition.

properties.name: muteRecognizerBeep
properties.docs:
Suppresses the Android system beep when inline recognition starts or restarts.

Uses a best-effort combination of an undocumented recognizer intent extra and
temporary notification/system stream volume muting. Some devices ignore the
intent extra; the volume fallback is the portable path.

Defaults to `true` when `continuousPTT` is enabled.

properties.name: timeout
properties.docs:
Android only: timeout in milliseconds before forcing stop via destroy/recreate.

On iOS, the current session is stopped immediately and this value is ignored.

Defaults to `1500`.

properties.name: available
properties.docs: Whether a partial result is currently cached.

properties.name: text
properties.docs: The most recent transcript text known to the native recognizer.

properties.name: matches
properties.docs: All current match alternatives when available.

properties.name: held
properties.docs: Whether the PTT button is currently held.

properties.name: mute
properties.docs:
When set, updates whether Android should suppress the recognizer start beep for the active session.

Beep suppression is best-effort and device-specific; see {@link SpeechRecognitionStartOptions.muteRecognizerBeep}.

interfaces.name: SpeechRecognitionPermissionStatus
interfaces.docs:
Permission map returned by `checkPermissions` and `requestPermissions`.

On Android the state maps to the `RECORD_AUDIO` permission.
On iOS it combines speech recognition plus microphone permission.

interfaces.name: SpeechRecognitionSegmentResultEvent
interfaces.docs: Raised whenever a segmented result is produced (Android only).

interfaces.name: SpeechRecognitionPartialResultEvent
interfaces.docs: Raised whenever a partial transcription is produced.

properties.name: matches
properties.docs:
Current recognition matches when the native recognizer reports them.

This can be omitted for forced or accumulated-only payloads.

properties.name: accumulated
properties.docs: Accumulated transcription from earlier continuous PTT cycles.

properties.name: accumulatedText
properties.docs: Final accumulated text including the current result.

properties.name: isRestarting
properties.docs: `true` when the plugin is restarting recognition inside a continuous PTT session.

properties.name: forced
properties.docs: `true` when the payload was emitted by `forceStop()`.

interfaces.name: SpeechRecognitionListeningEvent
interfaces.docs:
Raised when the listening state changes.

The original `status` field is preserved for backward compatibility and is present
on the binary `started` / `stopped` states.

properties.name: state
properties.docs: Finite state of the recognition session.

properties.name: sessionId
properties.docs: Unique identifier for the current listening session.

properties.name: reason
properties.docs: Why this state transition occurred.

properties.name: errorCode
properties.docs: Error code when the transition is caused by an error.

properties.name: status
properties.docs: Backward-compatible binary state used by earlier releases.

interfaces.name: SpeechRecognitionErrorEvent
interfaces.docs: Raised whenever native recognition reports an error.

interfaces.name: SpeechRecognitionReadyEvent
interfaces.docs: Emitted after native resources have been torn down and the plugin is ready for another session.

typeAliases.name: Pick
typeAliases.docs: From T, pick a set of properties whose keys are in the union K

typeAliases.name: ListeningFiniteState
typeAliases.docs: Finite state values for the recognition session lifecycle.

typeAliases.name: ListeningReason
typeAliases.docs: Why a listening state transition happened.
